title_en: Grade 1 Winner Zulu Kingdom Looks to Rebound in Kelso
description: Saratoga Race Course has provided a mixed bag of adventures for multiple grade 1 winner Zulu Kingdom.

title_en: Box Office Hit is First Winner for Idol
description: Box Office Hit wins her racing debut July 2 by 6 1/4 lengths at Thistledown for Mast Thoroughbreds and trainer Robert Gorham.

title_en: Constitution River, Hawk Mountain Meet Again in Eclipse
description: Aidan O'Brien's bid to become the first trainer to win the Eclipse Stakes (G1) four years in a row will be stronger than expected after he surprisingly revealed Constitution River and Hawk Mountain will both run July 4 at Sandown Park.

title_en: Heat Wave Sparks Cancellation at Colonial Downs
description: Colonial Downs is the latest track to cancel racing July 3 because of extreme summer heat gripping much of the country. The Virginia track has rescheduled its Friday card, moving it in its entirety as drawn to July 6.

title_en: Infrastructure Repairs Force Woodbine to Cancel July 3
description: Woodbine announced July 2 that racing July 3 has been canceled because of ongoing underground infrastructure repairs. Contractors remain on-site working to resolve the issue.

title_en: Book'em Danno to be Honored at Monmouth Park
description: Book'em Danno, a champion and multimillionaire, is one of the most successful New Jersey-breds ever. He will be honored July 17 with a Book'em Danno Day at Monmouth Park and is scheduled to compete the same day in the Mr. Prospector Stakes.

title_en: Savabeel Factor in National Online Breeding Stock Sale
description: A rare opportunity to secure the last-crop offspring by the late great Savabeel is available via the National Online Breeding Stock Sale on Gavelhouse Plus, with two mares in foal to the legendary stallion on offer.

title_en: Will Appleby Sweep the Belmont Derby and Belmont Oaks?
description: J. Keeler Johnson handicaps the Belmont Derby (G1) and Belmont Oaks (G1) at Saratoga.

title_en: Soumillon's Eight-Day Suspension Reduced to Five
description: Christophe Soumillon's ban for riding to benefit a stablemate is reduced to five days from eight on appeal after the disciplinary panel concluded that while his ride on Puerto Rico aided Gstaad, it was not his intention to do so.

title_en: F-T California Fall Sale to Offer $500K Incentive
description: Fasig-Tipton, in partnership with the CTBA, will offer a $500,000 bonus tied to this year's Fasig-Tipton California Fall Yearlings Sale. The bonus will be awarded to the first sale graduate that wins a grade 1 race in the United States at age 2 or 3.

title_en: Dettori in Hospital After Car Crash in Newmarket
description: A hospitalized Frankie Dettori said he is "very sore" after breaking ribs and his thumb in a car accident July 1 near Newmarket.

title_en: Moore, Moquett, Sims Join HISA Advisory Group
description: Chris Moore, Chance Moquett, and Blake Sims join the Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority's Next Generation Advisory Group.

title_en: Second Time's the Charm: Laurel Park Purchase Approved
description: Maryland's Board of Public Works approves the Maryland Stadium Authority's request to purchase Laurel Park to serve as the training center. This comes after the Maryland Stadium Authority supported the deal in April.

title_en: Fil Dor Seeks Second Top-Level Win in Leo O'Brien
description: Del Rio Racing's grade 1 winner Fil Dor is the 158-pound high weight as one of two entrants for trainer Ricky Hendriks in the $150,000 Leo O'Brien Stakes (NSA-1), a 2 3/8-mile steeplechase handicap for older horses, July 5 at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: BH Daily Letters to the Editor, July 2 Issue
description: Letters to the editor for the July 2 issue of BloodHorse Daily includes submissions mourning the losses of Aqueduct Racetrack and FanDuel TV.

title_en: Sewer Leak Forces Cancellation of July 2 Woodbine Card
description: Woodbine Entertainment announced July 1 live Thoroughbred racing for July 2 at Woodbine has been cancelled due to a sewer line leak at the all-weather main track.

title_en: Agenda Announced for Women's Horse Racing Conference
description: Olympic medalist Elana Meyers Taylor is the keynote speaker for this year's conference. Tickets are now on sale for the Horse Racing Women's Association conference, which returns to Lexington for the second year in a row and will be held Oct. 12-14.

title_en: International Fireworks Expected in Belmont Derby
description: On America's 250th birthday, there will be some fireworks from a lively international battle at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Update on Racetrack Fire Safety Review in New York
description: Following the devastating June 16 barn fire at Saratoga Casino Hotel, the New York State Gaming Commission is evaluating enhanced fire safety protocols and potential code changes to better protect horses housed at racetracks across the state.

title_en: Abashiri, Kensington Lane Imposing in Belmont Oaks
description: The Belmont Oaks (G1T), won four times by European-trained fillies in its last eight renewals, sees two invaders from across the Atlantic Ocean when the $600,000 race is renewed July 4 at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: New Jersey Restores $10M Subsidy for Monmouth Park
description: After an initial proposed cut to $5 million, New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill signed off on a state budget that restored the annual purse subsidy to $10 million.

title_en: Booked Primed for Sanford Breakthrough
description: Having the benefit of a win over the local track surface, the Steve Asmussen-trained Booked aims to repeat in the 6-furlong $225,000 Sanford Stakes (G3) for 2-year-olds July 4 at Saratoga Race Course.

title_en: Old Friends Announces Annual HayRaiser Campaign
description: Committed to providing the highest standard of care for its beloved equine residents, Old Friends will launch their annual fundraiser to help cover the costs of quality hay and forage that help keep their horses healthy year-round.

title_en: Mikki Fight Scores Repeat Win in Teio Sho on Oi Dirt
description: Mikki Fight continued a remarkably consistent career at the top of Japan's dirt-racing ranks with a repeat victory July 1 at Oi Racecourse.

title_en: Madaket Road Among Entries for Fasig-Tipton HORA Sale
description: Fasig-Tipton released the initial catalog July 1 for its July Selected Horses of Racing Age Sale, being held July 14 in Lexington. The sale will immediately follow Fasig-Tipton's The July Sale of selected yearlings that same day.

title_en: FTC Upholds Trainer Serpe's Two-Year Suspension
description: The Federal Trade Commission upholds a suspension imposed on trainer Philip Serpe by the Horseracing Integrity and Safety Authority.

title_en: Luminous Beauty Could Shine in Spa's Schuylerville
description: A $40,000 buy could become a black-type earner opening day of the 46-day Saratoga Race Course meet July 3, as Luminous Beauty tries to follow up an impressive maiden win in the $200,000 Schuylerville Stakes.

title_en: Resolute Thinking Big with Giant Sequoia, Goliath
description: Big-spending owner John Stewart believes the last week could mark a watershed moment for his Resolute Racing operation as he bids to establish it as a major new global force in racing.

title_en: Extreme Heat Causes Cancellations at Multiple Tracks
description: With severe heat forecast for July 3, Monmouth Park and Delaware Park cancel racing Friday.

title_en: Jose Ortiz Voted Jockey of The Week
description: Highlighted by two graded stakes victories on Stephen Foster Day aboard Lagynos and Magnitude, Jose Ortiz earned Jockey of the Week honors for June 22-28.

title_en: Safety Summit Highlights Racehorse Data Revolution
description: Equine professionals at the forefront of health and performance technology deliver insightful presentations regarding objective equine-monitoring data during the June 30 Welfare and Safety of the Racehorse Summit at Keeneland.

title_en: $3.4 Million Yearling Purchase Faran Breaks His Maiden
description: On June 27, $3.4 million yearling purchase Faran broke his maiden at Los Alamitos Race Course, while $1.4 million 2-year-old purchase Tagermeen broke his at Churchill Downs.

title_en: Magnitude Jumps to No.1 Spot in NTRA Rankings
description: Following an emphatic victory in last weekend's $1,998,000 Stephen Foster Stakes (G1) at Churchill Downs, Magnitude reclaimed the No. 1 spot in the June 30 NTRA Top Thoroughbred Poll.

title_en: U.S. Owners Buy Into Unbeaten Juvenile Adonius
description: Rebecca Menzies is dreaming of Breeders' Cup glory as she plots the next step for Adonius after the exciting gelding was part-bought by the Virginia-based Riverdee and West Paces.

title_en: Bowman Named The Jockey Club Steward at NYRA Tracks
description: Trish Bowman has been named The Jockey Club steward for the New York Racing Association racetracks beginning July 1.

title_en: Exploring Clever Pedigrees of Miss Vyvyanne, Lagynos
description: The pedigree match is not unimportant, but numerous examples exist of a clever pedigree pattern upgrading a modest commercial background. Recent graded stakes winners Miss Vyvyanne and Lagynos are good examples.

title_en: Hinkle Farms' Success a Century in the Making
description: Giulio's Jewel, dam of Deterministic, is just one aspect of Hinkle Farms' success. The family-run venture has been in constant evolution since the land was purchased by Anne Archer Hinkle's great-grandfather in 1926.

title_en: Grade 1 Winner, Sire Channel Cat Sold to Turkey
description: Channel Cat is a freshman sire by multiple leading turf sire English Channel.

title_en: Double Diamond Homebred Sire Bahamian Squall Dies at 17
description: Donald Dizney, owner of Double Diamond Farm in Florida, bred and raced the son of Gone West. Bahamian Squall beat grade 1 winner Trinniberg in the 2013 Smile Sprint Handicap (G2) and ran second in the Alfred G. Vanderbilt Handicap (G1).

title_en: Antiope First Winner for Freshman Sire Jackie's Warrior
description: Antiope scores in her career debut June 26 at Colonial Downs, becoming the first winner for Spendthrift Farm's freshman sire Jackie's Warrior.

title_en: Mystic Guide Sires First Winner in South Korea
description: First-time starter Seraph won her debut by 7 lengths June 26 at Busan Racecourse.

title_en: BloodHorse.com Launches Maiden Watch Landing Page
description: BloodHorse.com has launched a Maiden Watch landing page that will allow our readers to quickly see the horses we have highlighted in the Maiden Watch column that appears in BloodHorse Daily.

title_en: Prominent North Carolina Breeder Shuford Dies at 79
description: Nancy Shuford was honored as North Carolina Breeder of the Year seven times between 2013 and 2025 by the Thoroughbred Owners and Breeders Association. She bred 13 black-type winners, including six graded stakes winners.

title_en: Flight Command Soars, First U.S. Winner for Flightline
description: Flight Command became the first American winner for Flightline in romping by 10 lengths in front-running fashion in the fifth race June 25 at Aqueduct Racetrack.

title_en: Ontario Commits CA$35M Annually to Equine Industry
description: The Ontario government is increasing support for agriculture and rural jobs across Ontario by contributing an additional CA$35 million annually for the next five years. About 80% goes to support Ontario's breeding sector and racing participants.

title_en: Belleza Latina, Lovely Em Head F-T June Digital Sale
description: Fasig-Tipton has cataloged 283 entries for its June Digital Sale. The five-day virtual sale will offer a diverse lineup of yearlings, racing or breeding prospects, and accomplished broodmares.

title_en: Two New Members Elected to MHBA Board of Directors
description: Three incumbents and two new members have been elected to the Maryland Horse Breeders Association board of directors. The election results were announced June 24.

title_en: Joliestar May Target July Cup, Return to Ascot in 2027
description: Joliestar is set to contest Newmarket's July Cup (G1) July 11 and to try Royal Ascot again next year after running a brave but agonizing third in the June 20 Queen Elizabeth II Jubilee Stakes (G1).

title_en: Sarayu Becomes First Winner for Sire Collusion Illusion
description: Sarayu breaks her maiden June 20 at Woodbine, becoming freshman sire Collusion Illusion's first winner.

title_en: Social Inclusion Returning to Pennsylvania for 2027
description: Social Inclusion, the grade 1-placed son of Pioneerof the Nile, is returning to Pennsylvania to stand stud in 2027. He will relocate to Bonner Hill Farms near Littlestown, Pa., said Bonner Hill Farms owner Cristina Holmes.

title_en: Coolmore Celebrates 'Iconic' Day for Justify at Ascot
